name: The White House
subject: Momentum builds during a big week for tax reform
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=891c87ea6a3112c13834e33cd1d2733f639f3fcc179513e70e35cf4023af7869122f576bf7e8b7079aefadcc908334174f7a19a7a7f7a137

The Tax Cuts and Jobs Act moves toward the Senate floor

Yesterday just after 3:00 p.m. EST, Press Secretary Sarah Sanders released a statement commending the Senate Budget Committee for its crucial vote to pass the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act. The committee’s decision clears the way for the full Senate to vote on the bill as soon as the end of the week.

“President Donald J. Trump applauds the Senate Budget Committee on taking an important step toward passing historic tax relief and reform,” said the statement. “The President looks forward to providing tax cuts for hardworking Americans by the end of the year.”

The Tax Cuts and Jobs Act reduces taxes for families and individuals of all income levels, simplifies America’s complicated and lengthy tax code, and reduces taxes on U.S. businesses to allow them to compete – and win – on the global stage.

Read the
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=891c87ea6a3112c1484f02163ebb45ad50a78a13f80bd8b15461bd4ef42b1e04389a62f160c2a590a2ce3b04a319a64e33d3a53fdbae9946
full statement and
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=891c87ea6a3112c1250e2dfd2412378f1a868565cb89de1f495d2e51f9af074d95d43553ece9cdf59a79ec12ddd0b8e77ec050ebc0bc32a0
learn more about President Trump’s principles for tax reform.

A sneak peek at the President’s speech today in Missouri

Today President Trump travels to St. Charles, Missouri, to speak to Americans of all stripes about the crucial need for tax reform. The President will talk about overtaxation, a phenomenon that hits small-business owners especially hard by stifling opportunities to grow their companies. President Trump will also address the merits of the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act, which will allow more of these businesses to keep higher shares of their earnings and thrive in today’s ultracompetitive business environment.

St. Charles is a town of about 70,000 on the banks of the Missouri River. One of the oldest cities in the state, it’s the perfect place for President Trump to escape the Washington echo chamber and listen to the concerns of working Americans first-hand.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=891c87ea6a3112c1d4eabbe86cccee8122b7740654944614dee9a7750ed840ac8f4eb0db3d27f336a89e371f946dc25bd482297c5562d3b2
Watch the President’s remarks LIVE at 3:30pm EST.

Congressional leaders join President Trump in the West Wing

Speaker of the House Paul Ryan (R-WI) and Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell (R-KY) met with President Trump yesterday in the Roosevelt Room to discuss a plan to keep the federal government open past a looming December 9 deadline. The meeting was intended to be a bipartisan discussion, but Democratic leaders Nancy Pelosi (D-CA) and Chuck Schumer (D-NY) ultimately chose not to attend.

“I think it's regrettable that our Democratic colleagues and leadership chose not to join us today,” said Speaker Ryan. “We have important work to do. We have big deadlines . . . That works needs to happen now.”

President Trump will travel to Missouri, where he will deliver remarks on the pressing need for tax reform to stimulate the growth and prosperity of small businesses.

name: Team Kaine
subject: Become a founding donor today >>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
**There are just 33 days left in 2017, and only two days left in November.[Become a Team Kaine founding donor before our end-of-month deadline](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=YvcUZ15tE%2FIPGymgi0tlucNIQlSo2yt3), and we'll send you a Kaine 2018 sticker as a special thank you.**

**[CHIP IN NOW](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=Riqkpy6fNG%2F4s5A0%2FghzgSlft0%2F6Co8f)**
---
Derek,

There are just 33 days left in 2017.

Can you believe it? The year has flown by. Which isn't to say it's been an easy one: Tim has been fighting tooth and nail in the Senate to protect access to health care, prevent tax hikes on middle-class Americans, and champion bipartisan solutions to many issues facing folks today.

Although there have been some tough fights, during this time of year, we like to reflect on the gains we've seen and progress we're grateful for. That starts with you. Thanks to your hard work and generous support, we have been able to protect health care. (And we're working on stopping a bad tax bill right now -- if you're outside the Commonwealth, you can weigh in with your own Senators by calling (202) 224-3121.) We also celebrated major wins in Virginia earlier this month with the election of three strong Democrats, Ralph Northam, Justin Fairfax, and Mark Herring.

But while we think about both the challenges and accomplishments of 2017, we're getting ready for a quickly-approaching 2018. **Before you know it, we'll be in the thick of our re-election campaign during a tough year.** Republicans will want to continue their 2016 successes and make sure they keep the Senate in their control. But Virginians know that progressive leadership means more prosperity for all Americans -- and Democrats have a shot at a Senate majority if we all step up.

**[Will you pitch in $5 or whatever you can afford to become a Team Kaine founding donor?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=fDWh0n3g6bQ4ABytTsk%2FLClft0%2F6Co8f) When you donate before the end-of-month deadline tomorrow, we'll send you a Kaine 2018 sticker so you can show your Team Kaine pride on your car, laptop, or even bike!**

Thanks for your support -- it means the world to Tim, and it's what's going to push us to victory next year.

-Team Kaine
| [**DONATE**](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=DV6Dy89ZsuPoobqr9LVNiSlft0%2F6Co8f)
---
| Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
If you no longer wish to receive updates from our campaign, please [unsubscribe](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=YQdvTS4hVVb0TlZ%2BV8Og1ylft0%2F6Co8f).
---

name: Russ Feingold, LegitAction
subject: The time to push back
disclaimer_text: paid for by LegitAction, Inc
body:
[ Legit ]LegitAction

Friend,

A legitimacy crisis is undermining all facets of our democracy: our
campaigns, our elections, and most of all, our votes. And LegitAction
is fighting back on every front.

We’ve banded together in the face of these threats and transformed
them into grassroots action. Just this month, LegitAction supporters
have worked hard to reform our broken campaign finance system and
restrictive voting laws. And there’s more progress to come in
December.

But none of our progress is for keeps until we see the laws get
passed. In order to keep going, we must meet our November financial
goal before the month ends.

That’s why I’m asking LegitAction members like you to step up. Our
goal: raise an additional $9,455 before the clock runs out on
November.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/go/2149?t=1002&akid=415%2E356062%2ENuy-dN ]Help meet LegitAction’s November financial goal before the clock
runs out. We just need another $9,455 by tomorrow. Make a contribution
of $3 to do
your part.

Even with the legitimacy crisis spreading every day under the Trump
administration, LegitAction supporters made crucial progress in
November.

We demanded transparency by working to advance a vital campaign
finance reform bill in Congress. We fought for the weight of our votes
by pushing candidates in Connecticut closer to embracing the National
Popular Vote Interstate Compact. And we pushed to restore voting
rights to over 1.7 million former felons across California and
Florida.

We can keep fighting back on every front against this legitimacy
crisis, but only if we stay united -- starting by reaching our
financial goal by midnight tomorrow. That’s why we’re asking you to
make your contribution right now.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/go/2149?t=1003&akid=415%2E356062%2ENuy-dN ]Make a contribution of $3 today to help meet LegitAction’s
November financial goal before the deadline.

Together, we can provide the support necessary to restore government
to the people.

Thank you for standing up for democratic legitimacy today,

Russ Feingold
LegitAction

body:

| |

| One news story recently called this election my "toughest challenge yet." But let me tell you all -- it's nothing compared to the challenges Florida families are facing every single day. They're fighting to make ends meet. They're fighting to pay for doctor visits or medication; send their kids to school, or put a down payment on a house. **South Florida families elected me to watch their backs in Washington -- and that's exactly what I strive to do every day.** That gets me attacked by Trump and Republicans. It's gotten me four opponents in this race so far. That's fine by me. It's how I know I'm doing my job right. But when it comes to responding to the attacks and smears, I need your help too. [_**We have a deadline tomorrow night and another $2,371 to raise in order to reach our $11,000 goal. If at all possible, help us reach it by giving $5 or more.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113e7d/622b7b5b/7d13cac/307ba777/2094923427/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse171129ndam?refcode=dwse171129ndam_m&recurring=true&amount=5") I can promise you this -- whatever challenges lie ahead of me, I will take them on gladly. clean_body: If we’re being honest, we don’t even expect our senators to get their jobs done at this point. This whole year has been filled with disappointing inac Friend, need a perfect example of everything wrong with Washington? Look no further than the Senate. Remember when we the people voted to repeal Obamacare? ❌ The Senate failed to make that happen. Or when we voted for a secure border? ❌ The Senate has failed to make that happen. What about larger immigration reform? ❌ Big fat NO action from the Senate there, too. And who even knows what will happen with tax reform! If we’re being honest, we don’t even expect our senators to get their jobs done at this point. This whole year has been filled with disappointing inaction and incompetence. It’s ridiculous. And it needs to change. But the only way to really change Washington is to replace our lazy politicians with some new faces. That’s why I’m running for Senate and that’s why the Washington elite will do everything they can to stop me. But Friend, with you by my side I’m confident we can defeat them. We’re just hours away from a very important fundraising deadline and I need 29 more donors in your area. Friend, this race is going to be a tough one. The status quo never likes a shake up. But a shake up is exactly what the Senate needs. Please, help me bring conservative change to the Swamp and get Washington working for you again. Donate here in the next few hours to make a huge difference: Thank you, Friend. Leah Paid for by Leah for Senate

name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: UPDATE: Republican tax bill passes committee
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
Senate Republicans passed a tax plan through committee yesterday that increases the burden on the middle class and overwhelmingly rewards big corporations and the very wealthiest.

Meanwhile, the leader of the Republican Party - President Trump - refuses to condemn an accused serial child abuser.

[**Chris is working tirelessly to fight against this all out assault on the middle class. Will you help keep Chris fighting for us?**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91b86/70cdc2ba/46c8e825/39028f9/1045756211/VEsH/ "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/eom11?refcode=em112917&amount=25.00&recurring=auto&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+Chris+is+working+tirelessly+to+fight+aga&utm_campaign=em112917&source=em112917")

Read Chris' message below for more details. \-------------------------------------------------- Republicans are spreading a false narrative that their tax plan will actually help working people and that no one in the middle class will see a tax increase. That's dead wrong, and the American people deserve to know the truth.

Instead of standing up for what's right, Republicans are lining up to provide big corporations and the very wealthy with a massive tax break and leave the rest of the country to pick up the tab.

That's why I'm on my feet, fighting back against the crushing Republican plan. And it's why we need a Democratic majority in the Senate.

[**Will you stand with me to help elect strong Democrats to the Senate in 2018? We must raise $10,000 by midnight Thursday >>>**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91b86/70cdc2ba/46c8e825/39028f6/1045756211/VEsE/ "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/eom11?refcode=em112917&amount=25.00&recurring=auto&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+Will+you+stand+with+me+to+help+elect+str&utm_campaign=em112917&source=em112917")

Meanwhile, two weeks from today Alabama will hold an election where the choice is crystal clear. On the one hand, we have Doug Jones, a former U.S. Attorney who successfully prosecuted members of the KKK for the bombing of the 16th Street Baptist Church in Birmingham that killed four little girls. On the other hand, there is Roy Moore.

Simply put, Ivanka Trump had it right when she said "there is a special place in hell for those that prey on children." Unfortunately her father is supporting the child abuser.

[**I am doing everything I can to help Doug Jones in this special election, but I need your help. Please help me send a strong message to Donald Trump that Roy Moore is unfit to serve in the U.S. Senate. Meanwhile, the leader of the Republican Party - President Trump - refuses to condemn an accused serial child abuser. "" Read Chris' message below for more details. \-------------------------------------------------- Republicans are spreading a false narrative that their tax plan will actually help working people and that no one in the middle class will see a tax increase. That's dead wrong, and the American people deserve to know the truth. Instead of standing up for what's right, Republicans are lining up to provide big corporations and the very wealthy with a massive tax break and leave the rest of the country to pick up the tab. That's why I'm on my feet, fighting back against the crushing Republican plan. And it's why we need a Democratic majority in the Senate. name: Jay Inslee
subject: Over halfway there...
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
We're almost at the end of the month...

| [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MTI0NjIwMTcS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MjY3MjIwNQS2&mt=1&rt=0) Friend, I'm sure your inbox has been full of Trump this month. Trump planning to tax the middle class. Trump promoting big oil. Trump trying once again to gut Obamacare. Trump signing another horrific executive order. But not one policy that's going to make your life better, your country stronger. [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTI0NjIwMTcS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MjY3MjIwNQS2&mt=1&rt=0) [**Thanks to donors like you, we 're over halfway to our end-of-month goal -- can you help us hit it?**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTI0NjIwMTcS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MjY3MjIwNQS2&mt=1&rt=0) It's time your elected representatives worked for you. It's time we focus on what really matters -- not politics, not inflammatory tweets, but our common priorities, like promoting small business, protecting our future from the ravages of climate change, and ensuring a great education for our kids and grandkids. And that's exactly what I'm fighting for. Here in Washington, we've increased education funding, made international pacts to promote clean energy, and were named the #1 state for business by CNBC. Washington can be a model of how government can work for people and businesses alike. I want us to be a national leader -- because what we're doing for the climate and the economy is important for the entire country to see. Are you with me? [**With our $25,000 end-of-month deadline tomorrow, I need your help. Chip in to strengthen the Great Blue Wall and help make Washington a national model.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTI0NjIwMTcS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MjY3MjIwNQS2&mt=1&rt=0) Thanks for doing your part today. Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [CONTRIBUTE »](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=MTI0NjIwMTcS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MjY3MjIwNQS2&mt=1&rt=0)

name: Geoff Burgan, Andrew Gillum for Governor
subject: Everyday people
body:
#
Hello,

We've only got one day left this month, and Andrew needs your help to give everyday people a voice.

When he got in this race, he was doing it to finally give working Floridians a say in our government again. It's why he first got involved in politics at Florida A&M University -- standing up for fairness and representation in higher education and state employment against then-Governor Jeb Bush.

It's why he first ran for and won his City Commission seat at 23, becoming the youngest person ever to hold that seat. He did it to give everyday people in his city a voice and a vote -- a say against the entrenched powers. No one thought he could win -- but he shocked them all.

And that's what we're doing today in Florida in this campaign for Governor. We're running to give regular people a say on the issues that matter -- healthcare, public education, voting rights, the economy, and climate change. We don't care about the pundits or cynics -- we're in this to win.

**[We need you to step up now and help us meet our end-of-month goal of $8,000 -- will you chip in $5?](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb322d/9e00690/411ab5c9/24033de3/3011302550/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gillum-eom-nov?utm_campaign=em17_171129_EOM2ND_fnd_gq_ndn__GB&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&refcode=em17_171129_EOM2ND_fnd_gq_ndn__GB&utm_content=1+-+We+need+you+to+step+up+now+and+help+us+m")**

Andrew knows that everyday people need good-paying jobs so they can support their families and that it's crucial that we not only protect but expand, Floridians' access to quality, affordable healthcare as a right -- and not a privilege.

**[He has the experience and the vision we need to lead our state into a better future, but Andrew needs your help to give everyday people a say again next November -- chip in $5 and help us reach our end-of-month goal now.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb322d/9e00690/411ab5c9/24033de2/3011302550/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gillum-eom-nov?utm_campaign=em17_171129_EOM2ND_fnd_gq_ndn__GB&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&refcode=em17_171129_EOM2ND_fnd_gq_ndn__GB&utm_content=2+-+He+has+the+experience+and+the+vision+we+")**

Thank you,

Geoff Burgan

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: The Republican tax bill is a moral abomination
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Bernie Sanders
body:
This is not tax reform. It's an effort to radically transform American society and it must be defeated.
#
Sisters and Brothers -
For the past 40 years, the financial and political elite of this country have rigged the tax code to redistribute wealth and income to some of the richest and most powerful people in this country. The result: we are moving rapidly toward an oligarchic form of society in which the top 1 percent is doing phenomenally well, the middle class continues to decline and 40 million Americans are living in poverty.
And it will probably not surprise you to learn that just as our tax code benefits the wealthiest people in this country, it also benefits some of the largest and most profitable corporations in the world with a myriad of tax breaks, deductions, credits and other loopholes. As a result, one out of five large profitable corporations today pays nothing in federal taxes.
The current Republican "tax cut" bill, paid for by the Koch brothers and other billionaire campaign contributors, continues the push to make the rich richer at the expense of everyone else. It would raise taxes on middle class families making $75,000 a year or less and would throw 13 million Americans off of health insurance. And it would do all of these things to provide permanent tax cuts to the wealthiest Americans and profitable corporations that ship American jobs to China while moving their American profits to the Cayman Islands.
But let's be clear. This legislation goes well beyond taxes. Its ultimate goal is to radically transform American society and the role that government plays in the lives of the working families of our country. This legislation will increase the deficit by at least $1.5 trillion over ten years. Mark my words. If passed, the Republicans will then rediscover the "deficit crisis," and push aggressively for massive cuts in Social Security, Medicare, Medicaid, education - higher education in particular - nutrition, affordable housing and more. They will seek to undo every major piece of legislation passed in the last 80 years designed to help working families, the elderly, the children, the sick and the poor.
**This is the Republican plan. Huge tax breaks for the rich and powerful. Massive cuts to life and death programs for the middle class and working families of our country.**
**This is not moral. This is not what the American people want. This is not what our country and our pledge for "liberty and justice for all" is supposed to be about.**
**That is why I am going on the road this week to talk directly to working people in Kentucky, Ohio, and Pennsylvania about this disastrous piece of legislation. If we stand together - black, white, Latino, Asian American, Native American, male and female, young and old, gay and straight - we can defeat this horrific bill. But I need you to make your voice heard as well. We need to stand together.**
[**Please sign my petition calling on Congress to REJECT the Republican "tax cut" plan that would take from the working families in this country to give a massive tax break to people and corporations who already are doing extremely well. At a time of grotesque levels of income and wealth inequality, we must not make a bad situation even worse.**](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/bernie-gop-tax?source=em171129-no-full "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/bernie-gop-tax?source=em171129-no-full")
Today in America, more than 40 million Americans, including 20 percent of all children, live in poverty. Many in extreme poverty. Almost 28 million Americans have no health insurance. Millions of bright kids can't afford to go to college without facing a lifetime of debt. Seniors and disabled veterans are struggling to stay alive on inadequate Social Security checks.
Despite all of that pain, the greed of the billionaire class in this country knows no limits. No. We will not allow them to take away from those in need in order to give a trillion dollars in tax breaks to the very rich.
**Here 's a radical idea for my colleagues in the Republican Party: instead of just listening to the rich and powerful few in this country, maybe just maybe Congress should listen to the majority of the American people who want a fair tax system.**
Maybe just maybe corporate tax reform should start by preventing profitable companies from sheltering profits in tax haven countries like the Cayman Islands.
Here is something you may not know:
A 2008 Government Accountability Office report found that 83 of the Fortune 100 companies use at least one offshore tax scheme to lower their taxes. A 2016 study found that one of every five large, profitable corporations paid no federal income taxes at all in 2012.
The practice of stashing profits in places like the Cayman Islands has become so absurd that one single, five-story office building there is now the official legal "home" to more than 18,000 corporations! Our tax code has essentially legalized tax dodging for large corporations.
We must stop this bill. We must stop the Republicans from moving this country into an oligarchy.
And that starts with all of us standing up, fighting back and making our voices heard. Three weeks ago progressives from coast to coast ran for office at the local and state level - and they won. We have to continue that progress and build on that momentum.
[**Please sign my petition calling on Congress to REJECT the Republican "tax cut" plan that would take from working families in this country all to give a massive tax break to people and corporations who already have it all.**](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/bernie-gop-tax?source=em171129-no-full "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/bernie-gop-tax?source=em171129-no-full")
Brothers and sisters. We must do exactly the opposite of what Trump is attempting to do. He wants to divide us up by the color of our skin, our gender, our religion, our sexual orientation or our country of origin. He wants us fighting with each other while Wall Street and the billionaire class laugh all the way to the bank.
Our job is to bring our people together around an agenda that creates an economy and government that works for all, not just the 1 percent. Defeating this terrible piece of legislation will be an important step forward.
This bill is a moral abomination. I hope you'll add your name if you agree.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders

Sisters and Brothers - For the past 40 years, the financial and political elite of this country have rigged the tax code to redistribute wealth and income to some of the richest and most powerful people in this country. The result: we are moving rapidly toward an oligarchic form of society in which the top 1 percent is doing phenomenally well, the middle class continues to decline and 40 million Americans are living in poverty. And it will probably not surprise you to learn that just as our tax code benefits the wealthiest people in this country, it also benefits some of the largest and most profitable corporations in the world with a myriad of tax breaks, deductions, credits and other loopholes. As a result, one out of five large profitable corporations today pays nothing in federal taxes. The current Republican "tax cut" bill, paid for by the Koch brothers and other billionaire campaign contributors, continues the push to make the rich richer at the expense of everyone else. It would raise taxes on middle class families making $75,000 a year or less and would throw 13 million Americans off of health insurance. And it would do all of these things to provide permanent tax cuts to the wealthiest Americans and profitable corporations that ship American jobs to China while moving their American profits to the Cayman Islands. But let's be clear. This legislation goes well beyond taxes. They will seek to undo every major piece of legislation passed in the last 80 years designed to help working families, the elderly, the children, the sick and the poor. This is the Republican plan. name: Shannon Jackson, Our Revolution
subject: Did you see last night's debate?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY OUR REVOLUTION
body:
Last night, it appeared Ted Cruz forgot which tax bill he was talking about on the CNN debate stage.
#
Friends,
Last night, it appeared Ted Cruz forgot which tax bill he was talking about on the CNN debate stage. It wasn't quite Rick Perry forgetting which federal agencies he would eliminate if elected president, but it was close.
Bernie asked him, "Which tax bill are you talking about, Ted?" While touting benefits to the middle and working class, Senator Cruz seemed to forget about the $1.4 trillion deficit increase incurred by slashing corporate taxes. More than sixty percent of the tax benefits go to the top one percent. We know how this movie ends. Republicans increase the deficit to pay back their campaign contributors and then make a fuss about lowering it by decimating Medicare, Medicaid, Social Security, nutrition, education, and other vital programs. The only way we can prevent this from happening is if we organize.
[**Our Revolution is fighting for tax fairness through grassroots organizing. Can you contribute $5 to help us support the 500 local Our Revolution chapters across the country before our end-of-month deadline at midnight tomorrow?**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ae0d/bed43eb/285c9201/75ff66bc/3887184351/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-eom?refcode=em171129-suba")
Republicans like Ted Cruz love to talk about Ronald Reagan. It's almost like they have completely forgotten about the Bush administration--or they don't want the American people to remember. The same trickle-down policies they are trying to pass now led to the Wall Street crash and Great Recession, and progressives like Bernie saw it coming all along.
We cannot allow our democracy to be further undermined. We cannot allow more handouts to millionaires and billionaires at the expense of hard-working Americans. For years the DC political elite have been trying to install a corporate oligarchy at the top of our society made up of their billionaire campaign contributors. To them, if you can't contribute to their campaign then your interests are irrelevant. What they don't understand is that when we work together, the people have the power. Our organizing is the only counter to Ted Cruz's lies about helping the middle class.
[**Help us defeat this tax scam bill and build our organizing efforts by making a $5 contribution to Our Revolution today.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ae0d/bed43eb/285c9201/75ff66bc/3887184351/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-eom?refcode=em171129-suba")
We aren't going to have the wool pulled over our eyes. We know exactly what the Republican Party is trying to do, and it's up to us to stop it.
In solidarity,
Shannon Jackson
Executive Director
Our Revolution

| | Peter,
